## Methodology

- **Tool:** k6 + custom Go benchmark harness
- **Environment:** 41-core, 35 GB RAM
- **Dataset:** 75M entities
- **Warmup:** 60 s
- **Duration:** 10 min per profile

## Results

| Endpoint | p50 (ms) | p90 (ms) | p99 (ms) | RPS | Error Rate |
|----------|----------|----------|----------|-----|-----------|
| /api/v1/endpoint-910 | 13 | 137 | 340 | 27752 | 0.40% |
| /api/v1/endpoint-350 | 44 | 139 | 455 | 44368 | 1.10% |
| /api/v1/endpoint-234 | 15 | 124 | 254 | 30124 | 0.80% |
| /api/v1/endpoint-228 | 48 | 134 | 232 | 5847 | 1.17% |
| /api/v1/endpoint-526 | 17 | 29 | 246 | 11570 | 1.28% |
| /api/v1/endpoint-535 | 29 | 46 | 143 | 32255 | 0.03% |
| /api/v1/endpoint-716 | 2 | 74 | 276 | 12675 | 1.39% |

### Flame Graph Interpretation

```mermaid
graph TD
  total["total: 1420 ms"]
  handler["handler.Execute: 1400 ms"]
  prepare["sql.Prepare: 45 ms"]
  query["sql.Query: 980 ms"]
  parse["parseRow: 120 ms"]
  marshal["marshalJSON: 180 ms"]
  validate["validateResp: 85 ms"]
  total --> handler
  handler --> prepare
  handler --> query
  query --> parse
  query --> marshal
  handler --> validate
  style query fill:#f96,stroke:#333
```

## Recommendations

1. Add composite index on (status, created_at) — estimated 60% improvement
2. Batch JSON marshaling with pooled buffers
3. Introduce read-through cache for hot entities (TTL 30 s)
4. Evaluate protobuf encoding for internal calls
